mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, tight labor market conditions signal strong consumer demand and wage growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 5-day returns in >50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in real personal savings (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate synchronized consumer liquidity and business confidence, which jointly fuel equity risk appetite and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth relative to the prior quarter reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which improves profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds the 1.5% threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of supply chain disruption.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms underlying economic expansion, which directly supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if GDPC1 growth consistently coincides with flat or negative 5-day SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or if the rolling Spearman correlation drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 2.5 percent, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces anticipated monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses above 2.5, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below chance levels over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A widening divergence where total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) rise while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls simultaneously signals a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts aggregate demand expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of historical windows, or the interaction effect becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate synchronized contraction in both manufacturing and construction sectors, which signals impending recessionary headwinds and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The dual-decline signal produces positive or flat 5-day SPX returns in >50% of occurrences, or the signal's out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.
